title: polypodium subpubescens
intro: Polypodium subpubescens is a species of fern commonly known as the "hairy polypody" due to its hairy rachises. This plant is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where its medicinal properties have been utilized for centuries to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Polypodium subpubescens include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems.
